Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine subject files and photographs, 1888-2008.
Collection Number: 24-4-3385

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine subject files and photographs, 1888-2008.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
24-4-3385
Abstract:
The collection contains materials relating to the history and founding of the College, administration and various departments including academics, the clinic, and buildings.
Creator:
New York State College of Veterinary Medicine
Quanitities:
37.5 cubic feet.
2 mapcase folders.
3.7 gigabytes.
Language:
Collection material in English
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY

The Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine was founded in 1894. It was originally named the New York State College of Veterinary Medicine.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

The collection contains materials relating to the history and founding of the College, administration and various departments including academics, the clinic, and buildings. Included are reports, notes, files, course materials, evaluations, handbooks, building plans, calendars, schedules, newspaper clippings, flyers, sketches, posters, photographs, rosters, sketches, directories, programs, postcards, photographs, slides, films, and guest books.
Also includes faculty biographical files.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine subject files and photographs, #24-4-3385. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:

Access to Boxes 1-22 currently unavailable.

Schedules (time plans).
Rosters

Building plans.

Item number: 5-57-74
Box 45 Folder 46
Professor Raymond R. Birch, DVM '12 working in the serum laboratory at the Veterinary Experiment Station. From the turn of the century, the College was a national leader in research to fight outbreaks of hog cholera, swine plague, anthrax, brucellosis, foot-and-mouth disease, tuberculosis, mastitis, and other infectious diseases.
 1921
